# HomeBurrow — Copilot Instructions

## Git policy

**Never commit changes automatically.** Always leave changes as staged or unstaged edits for manual review. Do not run `git commit` or `git push`. The developer reviews and commits manually.

---

## Project overview

HomeBurrow is a self-hostable private group sharing app for small trusted groups (family / friends / household). Core features: location sharing, group chat, file sharing.

**Full spec:** [`implementation_plan.md`](../implementation_plan.md) — read this before implementing any feature.

---

## Repository layout

```
HomeBurrow/
├── backend/                  # Python FastAPI backend
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── main.py           # FastAPI app entry, router registration, GET /health
│   │   ├── config.py         # pydantic-settings env config
│   │   ├── database.py       # async SQLAlchemy engine + AsyncSession factory
│   │   ├── dependencies.py   # shared FastAPI deps: get_db, get_current_user, require_password_changed
│   │   ├── models/           # SQLAlchemy ORM models (one file per model)
│   │   ├── schemas/          # Pydantic request/response schemas
│   │   ├── routers/          # Route handlers: auth, admin, groups, locations, chat, files
│   │   └── storage/          # File storage abstraction (base.py) + local disk impl (local.py)
│   ├── alembic/              # Database migrations (async env)
│   ├── alembic.ini
│   ├── manage.py             # CLI: python manage.py create-admin --email ... --password ...
│   ├── entrypoint.sh         # Docker entrypoint: runs alembic upgrade head, then uvicorn
│   ├── Dockerfile
│   ├── requirements.txt      # All Python deps
│   └── .env.example          # Required env vars with placeholder values
├── composeApp/               # Kotlin Multiplatform shared mobile code
│   └── src/
│       ├── commonMain/kotlin/dev/labstk/homeburrow/
│       │   ├── di/           # AppModule — central manual dependency injection
│       │   ├── network/      # Ktor HTTP client + API response models
│       │   ├── auth/         # Auth domain (login, token refresh, change password)
│       │   ├── groups/       # Groups + membership domain
│       │   ├── locations/    # Location sharing domain
│       │   ├── chat/         # Chat domain
│       │   └── files/        # File sharing domain
│       ├── androidMain/      # Android-specific code
│       └── iosMain/          # iOS-specific code
├── iosApp/                   # iOS entry point (Xcode project)
├── gradle/libs.versions.toml # Version catalog for all mobile deps
├── docker-compose.yml        # PostgreSQL + API services
└── .env                      # Local env config (not committed — see .env.example)
```

---

## Backend conventions

### Dependency pinning
- Always pin Python dependencies to **exact versions** using `==` in `requirements.txt` — never use `>=`, `~=`, or unpinned specifiers
- When adding a new dependency, install it and record the exact installed version from `pip freeze`
- This applies to all extras too (e.g., `fastapi==x.y.z`, not `fastapi>=x.y.z`)

### Async everywhere
- All database operations use `AsyncSession` from `app/database.py`
- All service/repository functions are `async def`
- Never call synchronous SQLAlchemy methods in async context

### Auth dependency chain
- Protected routes use `get_current_user` from `app/dependencies.py`
- `get_current_user` validates the JWT **and** checks `token_version` against the DB
- The `require_password_changed` dependency is composed into `get_current_user`; it raises `HTTP 403 MUST_CHANGE_PASSWORD` if `user.must_change_password` is true — except for `POST /auth/change-password` and `POST /auth/logout`
- Never enforce `must_change_password` per-handler; it is structural

### Authorization rules (per endpoint)
- Group data always requires membership check
- Group-owner actions (add/remove members) require `role = owner` OR `is_admin = true`
- Admin-only actions (create users, list users, reset password) require `is_admin = true`
- See `implementation_plan.md` §9 for the full API contract

### Token strategy
- Access tokens: short-lived JWTs with `token_version` claim (15 min default)
- Refresh tokens: long-lived (30 days), stored **hashed** in `refresh_tokens` table
- On every password change or admin reset: increment `user.token_version`, revoke all refresh tokens for that user

### Data model notes
- `current_locations` has a **composite primary key `(user_id, group_id)`** — use upsert logic
- `files.storage_key` is an **opaque UUID** generated by the storage layer, never a filesystem path
- All FKs referencing `groups.id` use `RESTRICT` (no cascade delete)
- `users.token_version` is an integer, default 0

### File storage abstraction
- All file operations go through `StorageService` in `app/storage/base.py`
- Handlers and domain logic must not import from `app/storage/local.py` directly
- `generate_key()` returns a UUID string; the local impl maps it to `<STORAGE_PATH>/<uuid>`
- Replacing the storage backend must require zero changes in routers or domain logic

### Migrations
- All migrations live in `backend/alembic/versions/`
- Alembic is configured for async (asyncpg)
- The `entrypoint.sh` runs `alembic upgrade head` before starting uvicorn — this is the Docker migration strategy

### Admin bootstrap
- First admin is created via `python manage.py create-admin --email ... --password ...`
- The command is idempotent (re-running does not create a duplicate)
- This must be the first step before any API interaction

### Error responses
- Use a consistent JSON error envelope across all endpoints
- Machine-readable codes (e.g. `MUST_CHANGE_PASSWORD`, `LAST_OWNER`) are required for errors that clients must handle programmatically

---

## Mobile conventions

### Dependency injection
- Manual DI with a central `AppModule` in `di/AppModule.kt` — no DI framework
- `AppModule` wires all dependencies at app startup

### Networking
- Ktor HTTP client configured in `network/ApiClient.kt`
- All API models live in `network/models/`
- Client intercepts `HTTP 401` to call `POST /auth/refresh`, then retries the original request
- Client handles `HTTP 403 MUST_CHANGE_PASSWORD` by navigating to the change-password screen

### State management
- Use `ViewModel` + `StateFlow` / `Flow` for UI state
- Coroutines for async operations

### Platform-specific code
- Location APIs go in `androidMain` and `iosMain` (expect/actual pattern)
- Storage (secure token persistence) goes in platform-specific source sets

---

## Implementation phases

Work is phased per `implementation_plan.md`. Stub files marked `# TODO Phase N:` indicate what gets built in each phase:

| Phase | Scope |
|---|---|
| 0 | FastAPI app, health endpoint, async DB setup, Alembic initial migration, storage abstraction, admin bootstrap CLI, config, auth dependency |
| 1 | Auth endpoints (login/refresh/logout/me/change-password), JWT + token_version, password hashing, admin user management, mobile token handling |
| 2 | Groups + membership endpoints, authz rules, mobile groups screen |
| 3 | Location sharing endpoints, mobile map screen |
| 4 | Chat endpoints + pagination, mobile chat screen |
| 5 | File upload/download via storage abstraction, mobile files screen |
| 6 | Self-hosting docs, `.env.example`, deployment notes |
| 7 | Hardening: input validation, auth checks, rate limiting, tests |

---

## Local development

```sh
# Backend
cd backend
source .venv/bin/activate
uvicorn app.main:app --reload

# Full stack
docker-compose up --build

# Admin bootstrap (after first docker-compose up)
docker-compose exec api python manage.py create-admin --email admin@example.com --password <password>
```

---

## Key concerns to keep in mind

These are explicitly called out in `implementation_plan.md` §15 and must not be overlooked:

- **CONCERN-1** `current_locations` must use composite PK `(user_id, group_id)` — verify in ORM model and upsert logic
- **CONCERN-2** Location sharing toggle is **per-group** (`PUT /groups/{group_id}/me/location-sharing`), not global
- **CONCERN-3** Admin password reset must return the plain-text password once in the response (never stored)
- **CONCERN-4** All FKs on `groups.id` use RESTRICT — document in migration comments
- **CONCERN-5** Removing last group owner must be rejected with HTTP 409
- **CONCERN-7** Message pagination uses cursor-based `?before=<message_id>&limit=<n>` — no offset pagination
- **CONCERN-8** Storage keys are opaque UUIDs — never store or expose filesystem paths
